Privacy Policy — Signal Messenger

Last Updated: May 1, 2020

Signal is designed to never collect or store any sensitive information beyond what is strictly necessary to operate the service.

Data We Collect

We require a phone number to register an account. We do not store your address book contacts on our servers. We do not collect your messages, which are end-to-end encrypted and stored only on your device.

Data We Do Not Sell or Share

Signal does not sell, rent, or monetize your personal data or content in any way — ever. Signal does not work with advertisers. We do not share your information with third parties for advertising purposes. We do not perform cross-site tracking.

Security

All messages are end-to-end encrypted using the Signal Protocol, which is open source and independently audited. We use forward secrecy to protect past communications. Your messages cannot be read by Signal.

Retention

Messages are stored only as long as necessary to deliver them to the recipient. After delivery, they are deleted from our servers.

Your Rights

You can request a copy of your data at any time. You can delete your account and all associated data at any time by going to Settings > Account > Delete Account. Signal supports data export.

Global Privacy Control and Do Not Track

Signal respects Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals. Signal respects Do Not Track browser settings.
